mappings.rationaleAI: Design, construction, and maintenance of public infrastructure including water supply, sewage systems, and roads corresponds to basic engineering services (A), water-supply systems (A.1), and sanitation systems (A.2).
Automobiles as the dominant mode of urban transport correspond to urban transportation systems (A.3), with associated road congestion and pollution.
Movement of humans and goods via road and rail modes corresponds to urban transportation systems (A.3).
Machines for transporting people or cargo correspond to urban transportation systems (A.3).
Public health infrastructure for clean water and safe sewage treatment corresponds to sanitation systems (A.2) and sewage disposal systems (A.2.a).
Engineered waterways for urban water supply correspond to water-supply systems (A.1).
State-constituted bodies enforcing law and maintaining public order correspond to police technology (B.1) and crime control technology (B.1.b).
Thoroughfares for vehicular traffic correspond to urban transportation systems (A.3).
Physical systems enabling societal functioning — roads, energy grids, water supply, sewers — correspond to basic engineering services of the city (A).
Permanently enclosed structures correspond to design, construction, and maintenance of urban facilities (B.2).
Combustion phenomena and firefighting technology correspond to fire prevention and control (A.6).

The global demographic shift to urban residence with environmental, health, and social consequences corresponds to basic engineering services (A) and technological responses to new urban problems (C).
Barriers creating reservoirs for water supply correspond to water-supply systems (A.1).
Delivering built assets corresponds to design, construction, and maintenance of recreational facilities (B.2).
Introduction of harmful contaminants from human activities corresponds to control of air, water, land, and other pollution (C.1).
Discarded materials requiring collection, treatment, and disposal correspond to street clearance and refuse disposal systems (A.2.b) and pollution control (C.1).
Originated practical electric power distribution systems, corresponding to technology of electric power in the urban context (A.5).
Design and construction of urban structures including recreational facilities corresponds to design, construction, and maintenance of recreational facilities (B.2).
Communities of people living in defined places with physical infrastructure correspond to basic engineering services (A) and social services (B) of the city.
Scientific study of organisms and their environments connects to control of pollution and technological responses to urban environmental problems (C.1).
